name: Hermitage of the Virgin of the Head from Ronda
name_original: Ermita de la Virgen de la Cabeza desde Ronda
region_intro: Jogging around Ronda offers diverse routes through its unique topography, characterized by the dramatic El Tajo gorge and the surrounding mountainous terrain of the Serranía de Ronda. The region features deep valleys, imposing mountains, and cliff-edge viewpoints. Routes often combine historical elements with natural landscapes, providing varied running experiences. Best jogging routes around Ronda The most popular jogging route is Hermitage of the Virgin of the Head from Ronda, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes about 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local runners is Plaza de España (Spain Square), Ronda – La Merced Church (Ronda) loop from Parque Periurbano Dehesa del Mercadillo, a difficult 6.8 miles (11.0 km) path. This route explores areas near historical landmarks and natural parks. Local runners also love the Running loop from Ronda, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail leading through the town's surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour. Jogging around Ronda is defined by the dramatic El Tajo gorge, the mountainous Serranía de Ronda, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Ronda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 1700 runners have used komoot to explore Ronda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Ronda?
A: Ronda offers a wide variety of running experiences, with over 110 routes available on komoot. These range from easy loops to challenging mountain trails, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What makes Ronda a unique destination for runners?
A: Ronda's unique topography, dominated by the dramatic El Tajo gorge and the mountainous Serranía de Ronda, provides a stunning backdrop for running. Routes often combine historical elements like the Puente Nuevo with breathtaking natural beauty, offering diverse and inspiring experiences.

Q: Are there running trails suitable for beginners in Ronda?
A: Yes, Ronda has several running routes suitable for beginners. There are 8 easy routes available, perfect for those looking for a more relaxed jog or to enjoy the scenery at a gentler pace.

Q: Can I find challenging long-distance running trails around Ronda?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Ronda offers 39 difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38246064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Plaza Pruna – La Merced Church (Ronda) loop from Ronda&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 40 km with significant elevation changes, exploring the expansive mountainous terrain of the Serranía de Ronda.

Q: Are there running routes with spectacular viewpoints in Ronda?
A: Many running routes in Ronda feature incredible viewpoints. The town itself is perched on cliffs overlooking the El Tajo gorge, and trails often lead to various &lt;em&gt;miradores&lt;/em&gt; (viewpoints) like Mirador del Viento, offering panoramic vistas of the gorge, the iconic Puente Nuevo, and the surrounding mountains.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or natural attractions to see while running in Ronda?
A: Yes, Ronda's routes are rich with sights. You can run past the famous Puente Nuevo bridge, explore paths leading into the El Tajo gorge, or discover ancient constructions in the Serranía de Ronda. Nearby attractions also include historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6277636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondragón Palace&lt;/a&gt; and natural wonders like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/857563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cat Cave&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into longer runs or explored separately.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Ronda?
A: Yes, many of Ronda's running trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5287123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Ronda&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.4 km trail through the town's surroundings.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Ronda?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for running in Ronda. The weather is milder, making it comfortable to explore the trails without the intense heat of summer or the cooler temperatures of winter. This allows for the best enjoyment of the natural landscapes and historical sites.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Ronda?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Ronda, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 30 reviews. Runners frequently praise the dramatic scenery of the El Tajo gorge, the diverse terrain of the Serranía de Ronda, and the unique combination of natural beauty with historical charm that the routes offer.

Q: Are the running trails in Ronda suitable for families?
A: While some routes involve challenging terrain, there are easier trails and sections within the Alameda del Tajo park that are suitable for families looking for a lighter jog or a scenic walk. It's advisable to check the difficulty grade and distance of a route before heading out with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Ronda?
A: Many natural trails in the Serranía de Ronda are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy a run with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural parks like Sierra de Grazalema or Sierra de las Nieves, and to be mindful of local regulations and wildlife.

Q: How accessible are the running trails in Ronda by public transport or car parking?
A: Ronda is well-connected, and many trails are accessible from the town center, making them convenient for those using public transport. For routes further afield, there are often designated parking areas near trailheads. It's best to check specific route details for parking information or public transport links to the starting points.
tips: On the way to the chapel Ermita rupestre de la Virgen de la Cabeza you can see the beautiful town of Ronda from afar.

On the way a nice view of the city of Ronda divided by a gorge and connected by a bridge. The road ends at a fence with the indication of the religious place, there you have to follow the paved path that descends steeply for 200m and you will find the site carved into the rock behind a newer chapel. The area is not accessible.

waypoints.name: Cave Church of Our Lady of La Cabeza
